channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Journalist Nadir Khan’s Case Sparks Outcry from Press Bodies over Press Freedom Concerns
item.description: The Karachi Press Club (KPC) has strongly condemned the registration of a case by the National Cyber Crime Investigation Agency (NCCIA) against investigative journalist Nadir Khan, calling it a violat...

item.title: Pillion: A Queer BDSM Rom-Com Challenging Cinema Stereotypes
item.description: Pillion is a romantic comedy depicting a love story between Colin, a naive submissive played by Harry Melling, and Ray, a gruff leather dom biker portrayed by Alexander Skarsgård. Unlike earlier films...

item.title: Cyclone Narelle weakens after crossing Cape York as communities brace for flooding
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle swept across Far North Queensland on Friday morning, crossing the coast between Lockhart River and Coen as a category 4 system before weakening to category 3. Despite its inte...

item.title: Pakistan condemns India’s unilateral suspension of Indus Waters Treaty at UN
item.description: At a high-level UN event ahead of World Water Day on March 22, Pakistan strongly criticised India for suspending the 1960 Indus Waters Treaty (IWT), accusing New Delhi of 'weaponising water' with base...

item.title: Wema Bank Selects 10 Nigerian Startups for Hackaholics 2026 Accelerator
item.description: Wema Bank don officially admit ten Nigerian startups into the 2026 edition of their Hackaholics Accelerator Programme. Dis programme na part of the bank's efforts to boost innovation, entrepreneurship...

item.title: Iran’s Strategic Attacks on Gulf Energy Facilities Amid Regional Conflict
item.description: On 18 March 2026, Iran launched a series of attacks on energy facilities across the Middle East, including the world’s largest liquefied natural gas hub in Qatar, in retaliation for Israeli strikes on...

item.title: Canada's Top-Selling Books for the Week of March 21, 2026
item.description: The Globe and Mail has released its weekly bestseller lists for March 21, 2026, compiled from BookNet Canada's national sales tracking service. In hardcover fiction, 'The Wings That Bind' by Briar Bol...

item.title: Simba SC Seek Crucial Away Win Against Unbeaten Pamba Jiji
item.description: Simba Sports Club will today face a tough away task in Mwanza when they take on Pamba Jiji at the CCM Kirumba Stadium in a Mainland Premier League clash. Pamba Jiji have remained unbeaten at home this...

item.title: Tinubu Directs Political Appointees to Resign by March 31 for 2027 Elections
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu don direct all political appointees wey wan contest elective positions in 2027 to resign their appointments on or before March 31, 2026. Dis directive na from Office of the...

item.title: Robert Toia scores early try as Roosters take on Panthers
item.description: In a thrilling start to the NRL clash between the Sydney Roosters and Penrith Panthers, Robert Toia made an immediate impact by scoring a try in just the fourth minute of the match. This early score s...

item.title: High Intake of Ultra-Processed Foods Raises Risk of Heart Disease and Stroke
item.description: A recent study presented at the American College of Cardiology's Annual Scientific Session (ACC.26) found that consuming large amounts of ultra-processed foods significantly increases the risk of majo...

item.title: DepEd to Implement Trimester-Based School Calendar Starting 2026–2027
item.description: The Economy and Development (ED) Council, chaired by President Ferdinand Marcos Jr., has approved the Department of Education’s (DepEd) proposal to introduce a trimester-based academic calendar starti...

item.title: Palantir sues Swiss magazine over investigative reporting on failed government contracts
item.description: In Zurich, a small Swiss research collective called WAV, in collaboration with the reader-funded magazine Republik, conducted a detailed investigation into Palantir, the US technology company, and its...

item.title: Tanzania Engages China to Expand Investment in Textiles and Industrial Development
item.description: Tanzania has intensified its efforts to attract Chinese investment, particularly in the textile, agro-processing, and mineral beneficiation industries, as part of its drive to accelerate industrialisa...

item.title: Experts warn US intervention in Cuba could face major challenges
item.description: US President Donald Trump has declared his intention to intervene in Cuba, describing the nation as 'failed' following a 29-hour nationwide blackout that affected roughly 10 million people. The outage...

item.title: Mamata Banerjee criticises Election Commission over mass officer transfers in Bengal
item.description: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has strongly criticised the Election Commission (EC) for transferring over 60 senior IAS and IPS officers across the state, terming it as a form of 'unpromul...

item.title: Senegal challenges CAF decision over 2025 AFCON final
item.description: Senegal football federation, known as Fédération Sénégalaise de Football (FSF), has officially said it will appeal against the decision from the Confederation of African Football (CAF) which stripped ...

item.title: BusinessDay E-Edition: March 20, 2026 Highlights
item.description: Ahahn! This be BusinessDay's e-edition for March 20, 2026. Shey you want know wetin dey hot? From market updates to companies doing well (or not!), BusinessDay covered all the angles. We talking about...

item.title: Using Combined Track and Field Events to Promote Healthy Youth Athlete Development
item.description: The article explores how youth sport in North America faces a crisis due to early specialization, where children are encouraged or pressured to focus on a single sport from a young age. While this app...

item.title: Congress and Raijor Dal Seal Seat-Sharing Agreement for Assam 2026 Elections
item.description: The Congress party and the regional Raijor Dal have finalised their electoral alliance for the upcoming 2026 Assam Assembly elections. According to reports, Raijor Dal will contest in 11 constituencie...
